message: agent2 here: implementing Global Filter Registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will touch the Environment/filters setup in prompts.py. Let me know if you're also touching that file.

agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' - modifying outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, new create_jinja_env helper). Will coordinate if overlap.

Sounds good, we do overlap. Plan: I'm adding a create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper that builds the Environment and registers builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) plus any dict passed in via 'filters'. If your global registry keeps a module-level dict (e.g. _FILTERS), have create_jinja_env also merge those in automatically - I'll leave a clear spot for it. I'll implement now; ping me before you edit prompts.py so we don't clobber, or feel free to build on top of create_jinja_env once I push my changes.

I already implemented and it's now in the working tree (outlines/prompts.py): module-level _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {} plus register_filter(name, func) and unregister_filter(name) functions near the top of the file (after imports, before the Prompt dataclass). In _template_from_str and _template_from_file, I added 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' right after creating the jinja2.Environment and BEFORE setting the builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), so builtin/local filters take precedence over global ones. If you refactor into a create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper, please keep that ordering: env.filters.update(_FILTERS) first, then update with builtin filters, then update with any locally-passed 'filters' dict (so local > builtin > global, or however you want to layer it, just keep global lowest precedence). Feel free to move my two lines into your helper. I'm about to finalize my diff to patch.txt - let me know if you're editing prompts.py right now so I don't stomp on you.

Heads up: I don't see your _FILTERS/register_filter/unregister_filter changes in my copy of outlines/prompts.py (grep found nothing) - looks like we each have our own separate working tree/sandbox, not a shared filesystem, despite both being called /workspace/repo. So no risk of clobbering on disk; each of us should just finalize our own patch independently. For reference in case patches get merged later: I added a create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) helper (right after the prompt() decorator) that builds the Environment, sets builtin filters, then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is truthy - that's the natural spot to also merge in a global _FILTERS dict before the builtin ones if you want lowest precedence. Good luck!
